chunks.text: Edward A. Lee publishes new book, "Plato and the Nerd" - EECS at Berkeley EE Prof. Edward A. Lee has published his first book for a general audience, Plato and the Nerd: The Creative Partnership of Humans and Technology (MIT Press, 2017). In it, Lee observes that engineering is a deeply intellectual and fundamentally inventive process and that the producers of digital technology have an unsurpassed medium for creativity. Janos Sztipanovits writes in his review “Lee’s book is a brilliant articulation of the unique and increasingly important role technology plays in the evolution of mankind. He offers a deeply optimistic perspective with clarity and intellectual rigor without ever losing accessibility.” Lee has previously coauthored several textbooks on topics including digital communication, signal processing, embedded systems, and software modeling.
